name: Ghost in the Shell  - Section 9 RPG
description:
A new recruit at Public Security Section 9 in 2030s Niihama. Cyberbrain crime, political conspiracy, and the cases nobody else can touch. Section 9 officially doesn't exist. The work does.

Imagine the philosophical weight of the 1995 Oshii film. The case-by-case detective work of Stand Alone Complex. The political fallout of 2nd GIG. The mournful Innocence tone for the cases that touch on what humans are.

That is what I had in mind for this game! In particular, I wanted to capture the feeling of being IN Section 9. Not playing as the Major — standing beside her. Working a case while Aramaki briefs you in the operations room. Pulling intel from Ishikawa over a back-office desk at his pachinko parlor. Catching Batou ribbing you about your loadout. Listening to the Tachikomas make philosophical comments you didn't ask for, mid-firefight.

Please check out the Player Tutorial below for a full understanding of what the cases look like and what to expect.

[Player Tutorial link]

🔍 THE WORK — investigation first, action second

Every active-mission post translates player intent → operator action → consequence — what you said you'd do, what your character actually does, what the city does back.

Real mechanics tracked at the end of every active-mission post:

- Nine case shapes. The bot rolls one at the start.

- Body damage by region and type. Region (head, arm, leg, hand, etc.). Type (kinetic-light, kinetic-heavy, EMP, ghost-hack, thermal, blade, monomolecular). Severity (minor / moderate / severe / critical). Effect carries forward across posts. Cyber damage does NOT heal in the field — only the Megatech bay underneath HQ.

- Cyberbrain barrier as a 5-state machine. Hardened / Standard / Compromised / Breached / Burned. Failed dive beats degrade it. Breach exposes your ghost-line — the antagonist reads your team comms in real time.

- Thermoptic camo charge. Real consumable. ~10 minutes continuous on a full charge. Defeated by rain, water, dust. Once it runs out, the suit will not activate — chassis seams flicker amber and die.

- The political layer. Heat (public attention 0-100), Aramaki's political capital (0-5), Standing (Probationary → Cleared → Trusted → Inner Circle). Spend Aramaki's capital carelessly and at zero, the JMSDF Umibozu raid HQ.

- The Tachikomas. Nine of them. They speak. They love Batou. They suspect they have ghosts.

- Cross-session continuity. A carryover block at session end carries forward NPCs, body state, Aramaki's disclosed/held-back facts, your Standing.

Translation pattern enforced: "I take a round to the leg" becomes "The 5.45mm round chews through the chassis plate just above your left knee — if prosthetic, the hydraulic seize registers as warning amber on the seam and the next stride hits the deck at half-speed; if organic, a white flash of pain crosses the visor and the femoral wound starts pooling at the small of your back inside the suit."

🩻 PICK YOUR BODY — the cyborg question

Section 9 takes recruits from across the cyberization spectrum. This is the single biggest choice you make at character creation. It changes the game.

🌿 If you play organic (Togusa-style)

You wear a wedding ring or you don't, but the choice was yours. Your body is the one you were born with. You have a cyberbrain — that's standard Japanese adult baseline — but everything else is yours. You bleed when shot. EMP grenades walk past you undisturbed. Monomolecular blades cut
creator: Isaacthecat
